description_long: This object is a large, yellow traffic light assembly designed for urban intersections. It features a tall, curved vertical pole with an extended horizontal arm, both painted in a bright yellow color. At the end of the horizontal arm is a three-lens traffic signal, with red, yellow, and green lights arranged vertically in a rectangular housing. A secondary, smaller signal is mounted lower on the vertical pole, also with three vertically stacked lights. The housings for the lights are boxy and have black trim around the lenses, which are slightly recessed. The structure appears robust and is likely made of metal for durability, with glass or plastic lenses for the lights. This type of traffic light is commonly found at major road intersections to control vehicle flow.
description: A large, yellow, pole-mounted traffic light with two sets of vertically stacked signal lights, one overhead and one lower on the pole, both with red, yellow, and green lenses.
description_view_0: The front view shows both the main overhead signal and the secondary lower signal, with all three colored lenses visible on each.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the depth of the signal housings and the slim profile of the pole and arm.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the back of the signal housings and the curvature of the supporting arm and pole.
description_view_3: The opposite side view again highlights the depth of the signal housings and the continuous curve of the pole.
